log.key: approval of a contract with the media center for cable casting services of public meetings for a term of three years and a not to exceed amo
log.title: Approval of a Contract with the Media Center for Cable Casting Services of Public Meetings for a Term of Three Years and a Not to Exceed Amount of $540,000 with Two One-Year Options to Extend for a Potential Not to Exceed amount of $900,000 over a five-year term, for cablecasting, production, and streaming services; CEQA Status – Not a Project.
log.draft: The City Council considers a three-year contract for cable casting services of public meetings.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'three-year contract' but the official item specifies a three-year initial term PLUS two one-year extension options for a potential five-year total. The headline omits the extension options, which are material terms of the contract being approved. This is incomplete/inaccurate representation of the official agenda item.

log.key: approve the city manager or designee to execute purchase order c26196045 with insight public sector, utilizing a county of riverside coopera
log.title: Approve the City Manager or Designee to Execute Purchase Order C26196045 with Insight Public Sector, Utilizing a County of Riverside Cooperative Contract ITARC-00930, to Procure Microsoft Enterprise Software Licensing, Cloud Services, and Server/Data Center Licensing for a 3-Year Term with a maximum Total Not-To-Exceed Amount of $2,506,936, inclusive of a Contingency in the Amount of $50,000 Annually.
log.draft: The City Council considers a software licensing purchase contract with a maximum cost of $2.5 million over three years.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto prohibiting parking of detached trailers and other non-motorized vehicles
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Prohibiting Parking of Detached Trailers and Other Non-Motorized Vehicles on Public Streets (FIRST READING: December 8, 2025; PASSED 6-1, Lythcott-Haims no)
log.draft: The City Council votes on an ordinance to prohibit parking detached trailers on public streets.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'votes on' which implies a decision outcome is uncertain or pending. The official item shows FIRST READING occurred on December 8, 2025 with a PASSED vote (6-1). The headline should reflect that this is a SECOND READING of an ordinance that already passed first reading, not merely 'votes on.' This misrepresents the procedural status and could mislead residents about where in the legislative process this item stands.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto prohibiting rental of vehicles for human habitation (first reading: decemb
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Prohibiting Rental of Vehicles for Human Habitation (FIRST READING: December 8, 2025; PASSED 6-1, Lythcott-Haims no) Public Comment
log.draft: The City Council votes on an ordinance that would prohibit renting vehicles for human habitation.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'votes on' which implies a decision is being made at this reading, but the agenda item specifies this is a SECOND READING following a first reading that already PASSED 6-1. The headline omits the procedural context that distinguishes a second reading from an initial vote, potentially misleading readers about the stage of the process.

log.key: study and assessment of palo alto turf systems and direct staff to proceed with el camino park synthetic turf replacement and natural grass
log.title: Study and Assessment of Palo Alto Turf Systems and Direct Staff to Proceed with El Camino Park Synthetic Turf Replacement and Natural Grass Pilot; CEQA Status – Categorically and Statutorily Exempt Presentation, Public Comment
log.draft: The City Council reviews turf system options and plans synthetic turf replacement at El Camino Park.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'plans synthetic turf replacement' as a definitive outcome, but the official item describes only a directive to 'proceed with' study and pilot programs, not a finalized plan. This overstates the decision and could be read as implying the outcome is settled rather than under deliberation. Additionally, the headline omits the 'Natural Grass Pilot' component, which is material to the full scope of what Council is reviewing, creating an incomplete picture that could be perceived as directional.

log.key: monthly investment report - november 2025 (unaudited)
log.title: Monthly Investment Report - November 2025 (Unaudited)
log.draft: The City Council reviews the investment report for November.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'reviews' which interprets the Council's action/intent. The official item states only that a report exists and will be presented (a factual object), not what action the Council takes toward it. A neutral, grounded headline should state only: 'Monthly Investment Report - November 2025 (Unaudited)' or 'City Council agenda includes Monthly Investment Report - November 2025 (Unaudited).' The verb 'reviews' adds interpretive content not directly supported by the item text.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 2100-2400 geng road [24pln-00356]: recommendation on site and design to demolish four existing commercial b
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 2100-2400 Geng Road [24PLN-00356]: Recommendation on Site and Design to Demolish Four Existing Commercial Buildings and Construct 65 Three-Story Buildings Containing 145 For Sale Townhome Units. Thirteen Percent (19) of the Units Would Be Deed Restricted to Low Income Households. The Project Also Includes a Conditional Use Permit for the Residential Use and a Vesting Tentative Map to Merge Two Parcels and Re-Subdivide For Condominium Purposes Which Council will Consider In Conjunction with the Site and Design Application. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on July 8, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt from CEQA in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15183 (Comprehensive Plan Consistency). Zoning District: ROLM (E)(D)(AD) -Research, Office and Limited Manufacturing (Embarcadero) with Site and Design and Automobile Dealership Combining Districts.
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board considers a proposal to demolish commercial buildings and construct 145 townhomes on Geng Road.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 3781 el camino real [24pln-00161]: recommendation on a major architectural review application to demolish c
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 3781 El Camino Real [24PLN-00161]: Recommendation on a Major Architectural Review Application to Demolish Commercial and Residential Buildings Located at 3727-3737 & 3773-3783 El Camino Real, 378-400 Madeline Court, and 388 Curtner Avenue, and to Construct a Seven-Story, Multi-Family Residential Housing Development Project with 183 Units, 13 Percent of Which Would be Provided at a Rate Affordable to Low Income. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on February 15, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt Under AB 130 (Public Resources Code section 21080.66). Zoning Districts: CN (Commercial Neighborhood) and RM-30 (Multi-Family Residential).
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish buildings and construct 183 housing units with affordable units.

log.key: conference with labor negotiators authority: gov. code section 54957.6; agency representative: sandra blanch and nicholas raisch, teri black
log.title: Conference with Labor Negotiators Authority: Gov. Code section 54957.6; Agency representative: Sandra Blanch and Nicholas Raisch, Teri Black, Teri Black & Co. Unrepresented Employee: City Attorney
log.draft: The City Council meets with labor negotiators regarding unrepresented employee compensation.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline adds interpretation not in the official item. The item states this is a 'conference with labor negotiators' under the specific authority of Gov. Code 54957.6 (which governs closed sessions for labor negotiations). The headline interprets this as the Council 'meets with labor negotiators regarding unrepresented employee compensation' — specifically adding the topic 'compensation,' which is not explicitly stated in the official item text. The item only identifies the participants and legal authority, not the subject matter of the negotiation.

log.key: overview and orientation to retreat focus city council retreat: discussion and selection of the 2026 city council priorities and ad hoc comm
log.title: Overview and orientation to retreat focus City Council Retreat: Discussion and Selection of the 2026 City Council Priorities and Ad Hoc Committees. CEQA Status -- Not a Project. Title Updated, Late Packet Report added, Public Comments, Presentation
log.draft: The City Council selects priorities and ad hoc committees for 2026.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'selects' (active voice implying decision-making completion), but the official item describes 'Discussion and Selection' as agenda items to occur at the retreat — the selection has not yet happened. This overstates what the meeting will accomplish rather than what it will discuss/consider. A neutral headline should reflect that the Council will discuss and consider priorities/committees, not that it will select them.
